title: Airbnb stock extends plunge, rival hotel shares also fall in aftermath of Russia's invasion of Ukraine
text: Shares of Airbnb Inc. ABNB, -9.20% took a 6.0% dive in premarket trading Thursday, putting them on track to suffer a fifth-straight decline, as Russia's invasion of Ukraine has triggered selloffs in travel-related stocks. The home-rental company's stock had already plunged 19.6% over the past four sessions.

title: CEOs of Nike, Hilton, and Chipotle top the list of overpaid bosses, according to a new report
text: At Chipotle, the ratio of CEO to worker pay was 2,898:1 last year, according to an investor advocacy group.

title: Hilton (HLT) Q4 Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates

title: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (HLT) Beats Q4 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (HLT) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 1.41% and 7.52%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2021. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Hilton swings to profit that matched expectations, revenue more than doubles
text: Shares of Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. HLT, +4.34% was little changed in premarket trading Wednesday, after the hotel operator swung to a fourth-quarter profit that was in line with expectations, while revenue more than doubled to top forecasts. The company reported net income of $147 million, or 52 cents a share, after a loss of $224 million, or 81 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Hilton revenue more than doubles as vaccinations, holiday travel boost hotel occupancy
text: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc posted a 106% rise in fourth-quarter revenue on Wednesday, as higher vaccination rates and a rebound in travel demand during the holiday period boosted occupancy rates at its hotels.

title: Will Travel for Pancakes: Complimentary Breakfast Tops List as Most Sought-After Value Travel Amenity
text: MCLEAN, Va.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Breakfast reigns supreme when it comes to travel, according to a recent consumer survey conducted by Hilton*, which found that complimentary breakfast is the No. 1 value amenity guests look for when booking a hotel. The survey revealed 73% cite unexpected food costs as a major pain point when on the road, and 40% of Americans have stayed at a hotel just to avoid cooking breakfast altogether.
